head.title: SQL Flight - T03
head.copyright: Audrey Anna Blein, Curtis Mellsop, Joel Mullarkey, Nicolas Neeser, Jibin Mathew Peechatt

text-scene.text: You have freed the pilot Arthur Tailor from captivity on SQL Island, good job! He agrees to fly you to the mainland on an old Airbus A320 - but there are limited seats. You need to choose your passengers wisely.
Arthur hands you the passenger manifest. Before you decide who boards, take a look at who is waiting on the runway.

select-scene.text: Challenge 0: Display all passengers from the Passenger table so you can see who is available.

text-scene.text: The flight costs at least 1000 coins per person, and you want passengers who bring useful skills for the journey. Let's check who qualifies.

select-scene.text: Find all passengers who can afford the trip (money >= 1000) AND have a skill. Remember to check the 'Schema' below.
select-scene.sql-solution: SELECT * FROM Passenger WHERE money >= 1000 AND skill IS NOT NULL

text-scene.text: Excellent! Marco, Sandra, Lina, Julia, and Aisha are boarding. The Airbus A320 roars to life and you take off from SQL Island!

select-scene.text: Before serving meals, you need a passenger overview. Count the total number of passengers, how many have a skill, and how many have allergies. Use the FILTER clause! Name the columns: total, with_skill, with_allergy. Hint: the syntax reads FILTER (WHERE ...)
select-scene.sql-solution: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE skill IS NOT NULL) AS with_skill, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE allergy IS NOT NULL) AS with_allergy FROM Passenger
select-scene.sql-placeholder: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, COUNT(*) FILTER (...) AS with_skill, COUNT(*) FILTER (...) AS with_allergy FROM Passenger

text-scene.text: 5 out of 8 passengers have allergies - that's a lot! You start distributing the meals carefully. The pilot Arthur grabs the Salmon Teriyaki from the galley before you can stop him...
Arthur collapses! The Salmon Teriyaki contains shellfish, and he's severely allergic! The pilot is unconscious. You need to take control of the plane!

select-scene.text: First, check the meal situation on our plane (planeid = 1). Count the total meals, how many are safe for everyone (contains IS NULL), and how many contain allergens. Use the FILTER clause! Name the columns: total_meals, safe_for_all, has_allergens
select-scene.sql-solution: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_meals, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE contains IS NULL) AS safe_for_all, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE contains IS NOT NULL) AS has_allergens FROM Meal WHERE planeid = 1
select-scene.sql-placeholder: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_meals, COUNT(*) FILTER (...) AS safe_for_all, COUNT(*) FILTER (...) AS has_allergens FROM ...

text-scene.text: OK, only 2 out of 4 meals are safe for everyone. You hand those out first. Now you grab the controls. The fuel gauge reads 62.5% - that gives you roughly a 700 km range. You need to find an airport to land!

select-scene.text: Emergency check! Count how many airports are reachable (distance > 0 AND distance <= 700) versus out of reach (distance > 700). Use the FILTER clause! Name the columns: reachable, out_of_reach
select-scene.sql-solution: SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE distance > 0 AND distance <= 700) AS reachable, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE distance > 700) AS out_of_reach FROM Airport
select-scene.sql-placeholder: SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (...) AS reachable, COUNT(*) FILTER (...) AS out_of_reach FROM Airport

text-scene.text: 5 airports are within range, 2 are too far. Now list them so you can pick the closest one for your emergency landing!

select-scene.text: List the name and distance of all reachable airports (distance > 0 AND distance <= 700), sorted by distance from closest to farthest.
select-scene.sql-solution: SELECT name, distance FROM Airport WHERE distance > 0 AND distance <= 700 ORDER BY distance ASC
select-scene.sql-placeholder: SELECT name, distance FROM Airport WHERE ... ORDER BY ...

text-scene.text: George F.L. Charles Airport on Saint Lucia, only 224 km away! You bank the plane hard to the left and begin your descent. Sandra uses her navigation skills to guide you, while Lina keeps the engines running with her mechanics know-how. The runway appears through the clouds...
TOUCHDOWN! You've landed the Airbus safely on Saint Lucia! The passengers erupt in cheers. Arthur is rushed to hospital. But the paperwork has only just begun...

The airline's insurance team rushes over with clipboards. They need a financial breakdown of the passengers immediately - specifically how passengers with useful skills compare financially to those without.

select-scene.text: Calculate the total and average money for passengers WITH a skill and for those WITHOUT a skill. Use SUM() and AVG() with the FILTER clause! Name the columns: skilled_total, skilled_avg, unskilled_total, unskilled_avg
select-scene.sql-solution: SELECT SUM(money) FILTER (WHERE skill IS NOT NULL) AS skilled_total, AVG(money) FILTER (WHERE skill IS NOT NULL) AS skilled_avg, SUM(money) FILTER (WHERE skill IS NULL) AS unskilled_total, AVG(money) FILTER (WHERE skill IS NULL) AS unskilled_avg FROM Passenger
select-scene.sql-placeholder: SELECT SUM(...) FILTER (...) AS skilled_total, AVG(...) FILTER (...) AS skilled_avg, SUM(...) FILTER (...) AS unskilled_total, AVG(...) FILTER (...) AS unskilled_avg FROM Passenger

text-scene.text: Insurance report submitted! Now the Saint Lucia airport authority wants a fleet-wide meal safety audit. They need to know, for every plane in the fleet, how many meals are allergen-free versus potentially dangerous.

select-scene.text: For each plane, show the planeid and count: total meals, how many are safe for all (contains IS NULL), and how many contain allergens (contains IS NOT NULL). Use FILTER together with GROUP BY! Name the columns: planeid, total_meals, safe_meals, allergen_meals
select-scene.sql-solution: SELECT planeid, COUNT(*) AS total_meals, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE contains IS NULL) AS safe_meals, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE contains IS NOT NULL) AS allergen_meals FROM Meal GROUP BY planeid ORDER BY planeid
select-scene.sql-placeholder: SELECT planeid, COUNT(*) AS total_meals, COUNT(*) FILTER (...) AS safe_meals, COUNT(*) FILTER (...) AS allergen_meals FROM Meal GROUP BY planeid ORDER BY planeid

text-scene.text: Fleet safety report filed! Arthur makes a full recovery at hospital. The whole crew is hailed as heroes. You've not only escaped SQL Island - you've mastered the FILTER clause! Congratulations, Captain!
